reasoning: Kiribati experienced 0 recorded riots over the preceding 360 days, meaning the threshold to resolve YES would be 11 or more distinct riots in a single 30-day period. As a very small, peaceful Pacific island nation of ~120,000 people, Kiribati lacks the civil instability to generate 11 separate riot events in one month; even their recent controversial national elections in late 2024 produced no recorded riots. I estimate the probability of massive social breakdown occurring and triggering 11+ riots is near zero, so I am submitting the minimum allowed probability of 0.05 for the near terms, and incrementally backing off toward 0.5 for the later dates simply as a nod to increasing uncertainty over longer horizons.
